pr.title: Match and link itdrmariopill, cleanup associated code
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched and linked the Dr. Mario pill item object by switching itdrmariopill.c from NonMatching to Matching, renaming many address-based pill functions to itDrMarioPill_* symbols, tightening fighter/item types, and moving pill-specific declarations out of the generic it_27CF header into itdrmariopill.h. The PR also updated symbol scopes for local functions/constants and connected the renamed callbacks through item logic tables and Dr. Mario/Mario fighter call sites.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itdrmariopill.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.

Renamed public Dr. Mario pill functions from address-based or generic item-logic names such as it_802C0510, it_802C0850, it_802C09C4, it_802C0DBC, it_802C0DF8, it_2725_Logic6_PickedUp, and it_2725_Logic88_* to itDrMarioPill_* names.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt and scratches.txt to reflect itDrMarioPill_* naming and to mark several helper functions and constants as local symbols, including @-style local data labels.
Moved pill-specific prototypes out of it_27CF.h and it_27CF.dox and into src/melee/it/items/itdrmariopill.h.
Updated fighter code that spawns or clears Dr. Mario pills to include itdrmariopill.h and call the renamed functions.
Changed ftMario_FighterVars.x2240 from u32 to HSD_GObj*, matching its use as a stored pill item object pointer.
Adjusted ftDr_AppealS_Anim local variables from generic u32 tmp to HSD_GObj* tmp plus int vitamin_idx, clarifying the object pointer versus vitamin index distinction.

Updated item logic table entries in it_2725.c so Dr. Mario vitamin callbacks point at itDrMarioPill_PickedUp, itDrMarioPill_DmgDealt, itDrMarioPill_Reflected, itDrMarioPill_Clanked, itDrMarioPill_Absorbed, itDrMarioPill_ShieldBounced, itDrMarioPill_HitShield, and itDrMarioPill_EvtUnk.
Reorganized itdrmariopill.c, replacing several temporary no_inline wrapper paths with direct named functions while preserving stack-sensitive matching details such as PAD_STACK where present.
smart_moves: The cleanup linked the object file only after renaming call sites and symbols consistently enough for build configuration to move from NonMatching to Matching.
Typing ftMario_FighterVars.x2240 as HSD_GObj* removed casts and made the Dr. Mario appeal pill cleanup path match the actual stored object semantics.
Separating vitamin_idx from the returned HSD_GObj* in ftDr_AppealS_Anim clarified two values that were previously both routed through a u32 temporary.
Moving declarations from it_27CF.h to itdrmariopill.h reduced generic item-header leakage and made fighter call sites depend on the specific item interface they use.
Changing helper function and constant symbols from global address names to local symbols reduced exported namespace noise once the translation unit matched.
Keeping explicit matching aids such as PAD_STACK(8) and comments about stack-sensitive JObj access shows the PR preserved decomp constraints while improving readability.
decomp_lessons: When an item file becomes matchable, update configure.py, symbols.txt, scratches.txt, headers, and all external call sites together; otherwise stale address-based prototypes can keep spreading.

Function names should describe the item and behavior where known, but unknown helpers can remain address-suffixed under the item prefix, such as itDrMarioPill_802C09C4, until behavior is better understood.
If a fighter variable stores an item gobj, correct the struct field type to HSD_GObj* rather than carrying u32 placeholders and casts through call sites.
Item-specific spawn and callback prototypes belong in the item-specific header, not broad catch-all headers such as it_27CF.h.
Matching cleanup can include symbol-scope cleanup: static helpers and compiler-generated constants should be local in symbols.txt when they are not intended external API.
Be cautious around stack layout in matched item code; this diff retained PAD_STACK and a comment that using GET_JOBJ in one location affected the stack.
naming_conventions: Primary item prefix adopted: itDrMarioPill_* with capital Dr, Mario, and Pill.
Behavioral names used where clear: Spawn, Appeal_Spawn, PickedUp, DmgDealt, Reflected, Clanked, HitShield, Absorbed, ShieldBounced, and EvtUnk.
Motion callbacks follow item prefix plus motion-state role, for example itDrMarioPill_UnkMotion0_Anim, itDrMarioPill_Motion2_Phys, and itDrMarioPill_Motion6_Coll.

Unknown public helpers were still allowed to keep address suffixes under the item prefix, for example itDrMarioPill_802C061C, itDrMarioPill_802C0B5C, itDrMarioPill_802C0DBC, itDrMarioPill_802C0DF8, and itDrMarioPill_802C1180.

The diff evidence shows a few remaining capitalization inconsistencies in names such as itDrmariopill_UnkMotion0_Coll and itDrMariopill_Motion2_Coll, so future cleanup should verify whether those are intentional matching constraints or leftover typos.
Compiler-local constants in symbols.txt were renamed to @-style local labels such as @165, @208, @209, @210, @211, @233, @255, @256, and @265.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Switched itdrmariopill.c to Matching after the code and symbol changes, contributing linked code and data gains reported by the bot.
Set many previously global item motion helpers to local scope in symbols.txt, matching their static declarations in itdrmariopill.c.
Converted global data/string/float/double symbols around the item file to local @ labels in symbols.txt to reflect local compiler-generated constants.
Preserved PAD_STACK(8) in functions where stack shape mattered.
Removed several artificial no_inline wrapper layers around pill setup functions and replaced call paths with direct named functions while still matching.
Used direct item state table wiring to static motion callbacks after renaming, keeping ItemStateTable it_803F75D0 behavior intact.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the supplied PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 linked code increased by 0.11% (+4224 bytes) and linked data increased by 0.02% (+224 bytes).
The bot also reported one unmatched-functions improvement for main/melee/it/items/itdrmariopill .sdata2, from 63.64% to 91.67%.
follow_up_queries: Audit remaining itDrmariopill or itDrMariopill capitalization variants in itdrmariopill.c and symbols.txt.

Investigate unknown address-suffixed helpers itDrMarioPill_802C061C, itDrMarioPill_802C09C4, itDrMarioPill_802C0B5C, itDrMarioPill_802C0DBC, itDrMarioPill_802C0DF8, and itDrMarioPill_802C1180 for more descriptive names.
Search for any remaining includes of it/items/it_27CF.h that only need item-specific headers.
Review other fighter item pointer fields still typed as u32 but used as HSD_GObj*.
Compare Dr. Mario pill callback naming with Mario fireball or Luigi fireball item naming for consistency.
